title: studio-desk single-port / production alignment + the :9100 sweep. A fresh browser at demo-N's studio-desk (e.g. http://localhost:39000) lands on a live page instead of a 302 to the dead :9100, by running the container's production code path. Root cause: the base platform docker-compose.yml ships NODE_ENV=development (which itself overrides the image's baked ENV NODE_ENV=production), and the demo override's per-frontend env block is ADDITIVE (deliberately not !override), so `development` survives -> src/index.ts isProduction=false -> the dev block runs res.redirect('http://localhost:9100/home'), a dead port. The fix pins NODE_ENV=production (+ FRONTEND_PORT=9000 belt-and-suspenders) in the studio-desk override -> the production sendFile path, no cross-port redirect. Plus the :9100 doc/CORS sweep (frontend-tier.md, demo-up SKILL, the un-offset 9100 CORS origin).
position_note: M32 is the 2nd and FINAL milestone of v1.7 'house lights' (M31 -> M32, strictly sequential — both touch the same demo doc cluster + the injection surface). After this close: v1.7 IS ready for /developer-kit:close-release (the user invokes it; this skill does not run close-release).
kind: a two-repo milestone. The CODE is in the ext authoring copy (rosetta-extensions @ tag house-lights-m32 @ 107599c; harden @ 7b17c39): the gen_injected_override.py studio-desk override NODE_ENV=production + FRONTEND_PORT=9000 pins + the backend-CORS 9100 removal + the test_injection.py regression assertion (test_studio_desk_env_pins_node_env_production, mutation-checked 4 ways, both content paths) + the CORS exact-set assertion (harden) + the 2 latent env-masked YAML-test fixes (M32-D3). The rosetta side carries the :9100 doc sweep (frontend-tier.md single-port story + CORS note, demo-up SKILL) + the planning artifacts. close-milestone's Phase 11 merge is rosetta-only (m32/studio-desk-singleport -> release/01.70-house-lights); the ORCHESTRATOR finalizes the ext side (ff main + re-point tag + delete branch).

field_bugs_surfaced.note: M32 is a tooling-change milestone (not a field-bake). No production code defects found at build, harden, or close. The build surfaced 2 LATENT env-masked test bugs (fixed Fate-1, M32-D3): test_frontend_blocks_parse_to_valid_compose's stale 29100:9100 ports assertion + test_a_plain_service_parses_to_ports_only predating the universal fix16/17 DIRECTUS_TOKEN= strip — both pre-existing on the M31 tag, masked because the YAML tests skip when PyYAML is absent. The harden pass closed ONE test-rigour gap (7b17c39): the CORS test had only membership asserts and did NOT pin the surviving set, so over-removing the kept 3001 next-web origin passed the whole suite (mutation-confirmed) — the new exact-set assertion catches over-removal + re-add. Neither was a production defect; the generator's emit was correct throughout.
field_bugs_surfaced.root_cause_note: The studio-desk dead-:9100 redirect was a REAL demo-UI defect (the milestone's reason-for-being), but it lives in the platform's src/index.ts dev path — M32 fixes it tooling-side via the NODE_ENV override (zero platform-repo edit), it was not introduced by M32.

notes: M32 is the 2nd and FINAL milestone of v1.7 'house lights' — the demo-UI-hardening release. M32 fixes the sibling studio-desk dead-:9100-redirect surfaced alongside the M31 browser-login investigation: a fresh browser at demo-N's studio-desk landed on a 302 to the dead :9100 instead of a live page. Root cause (verified by code-read + a close-time live merge-probe): the base platform docker-compose.yml ships NODE_ENV=development (which overrides the image's baked ENV NODE_ENV=production), and the demo override's per-frontend env block is additive (deliberately not !override, so inherited PORT/VITE_* survive), so `development` survived -> src/index.ts isProduction=false -> the dev block res.redirect('http://localhost:9100/home'). The 1-line-class fix pins NODE_ENV=production (+ FRONTEND_PORT=9000) in the studio-desk override to win the additive merge back to the production sendFile path (which covers every dev-block route via sendFile + an express.static(dist/public) mount + an index.html SPA fallback — NO route gap, #M32-D1), with the un-offset :9100 CORS origin dropped (dead now, #M32-D2) and the :9100 doc sweep across frontend-tier.md + the demo-up SKILL. The close-time observable verify was proven by composition (M32-D5: necessary+sufficient — the production pin is set [regression test] -> the production code path serves on the single 9000 port, no dead-:9100 redirect, no 404; demo-3 had been torn down so an end-to-end demo render wasn't re-spun). The close review found 4 findings, all Fate-1: 3 decision-tag blends (D1/D2/D4 into frontend-tier.md) + 1 adversarial-scenario record (the additive-merge scenario, defended live). Go 1027 unchanged (M32 touched no Go); Python stack-injection 87 -> 88 (+1 regression test); full suite 88/88 (0 skipped under PyYAML); flake 0; deferral audit GREEN. After this close: v1.7 is ready for /developer-kit:close-release (the user invokes it).
